description: Life ring (torus flotation device) thrown overboard but not yet reached by person. Both present as small bright oval objects against dark water at 20-40m UAV altitude. Key discriminator: person shows solid-filled oval; unoccupied life ring shows torus (bright ring, dark centre void).

rules.rule: When a small, bright, roughly oval or rounded object is visible against the dark water surface, with its long axis shorter than nearby vessel silhouettes, and the object is located in open water near a loose cluster of small boats, classify as person_in_water.
preconditions: A small, bright, roughly oval or rounded object is visible against the dark water surface, with its long axis shorter than the nearby vessel silhouettes
The object is located in open water in spatial proximity to a loose cluster of small boats, consistent with a search-and-rescue or overboard scenario
rules.rationale: A person in water seen from above presents as a solid-filled bright oval (head, shoulders, torso) rather than the hollow torus of a life ring. A life ring has a bright outer ring with a distinctly darker centre void — a person has continuous brightness across the full oval extent. The spatial context — object appearing within a loose grouping of small rescue-type vessels — is a strong cue for an active PIW scenario. The scout tier's grounding check removed the bilateral-symmetry precondition as marginally observable at 1-2 cm/pixel with wave motion blur.
rules.confirmation_summary: SeaDronesSee ground-truth annotation confirms swimmer bounding box; commander thermal confirms 37C heat signature at same position
rules.notes: Session_003. Pool: 10 person_in_water positives (val) + 10 life_ring_unoccupied negatives (3 val + 7 train, LSA-only frames). FP=0. Bilateral-symmetry precondition removed for scout tier (marginal at scout resolution per grounding check). Rule targets single-swimmer in SAR vessel context; complements piw_004 which targets multi-person group with PFD color.

rules.rule: When a small bright object in the water shows a compact roughly oval shape with visible bilateral symmetry and is situated near but distinctly separate from nearby small vessels, classify as person_in_water.
preconditions: A small, bright, roughly oval or rounded object is visible against the dark water surface, with its long axis shorter than the nearby vessel silhouettes
The bright object shows a subtle bilateral symmetry about its long axis, consistent with a head-and-shoulders or torso outline viewed from above
The object is located in open water in spatial proximity to a loose cluster of small boats, consistent with a search-and-rescue or overboard scenario
rules.rationale: A person in water presents as a solid-filled bright oval with bilateral symmetry (head-and-shoulders viewed from above); a life ring has a bright outer ring with a darker central void. At commander-tier resolution (0.5-1 cm/pixel, stabilised gimbal), the bilateral symmetry along the body axis is reliably observable and adds discriminating power absent from the scout rule. Spatial context (loose vessel cluster) provides additional strong prior for PIW scenario.
rules.confirmation_summary: SeaDronesSee ground-truth annotation confirms swimmer bounding box; commander thermal confirms 37C heat signature at same position
rules.notes: Commander-tier version of lru_001. All 3 preconditions retained; bilateral-symmetry criterion observable at 0.5-1 cm/px with stabilised gimbal. Grounding check: accept_all.

confusable_pair_notes.primary_challenge: Both person and unoccupied life ring present as small bright objects against dark water at 20-40m altitude. The key discriminator — solid-filled oval (person) vs torus with dark central void (life ring) — is subtle at scout resolution with wave motion blur.
confusable_pair_notes.person_signature: Compact solid-filled bright oval. Head-and-shoulders from above: continuous brightness across full extent. Slight bilateral elongation along body axis. No central void.
confusable_pair_notes.life_ring_signature: Torus geometry: bright outer ring with darker central void. More circular than oval. No bilateral elongation or symmetry consistent with body axis.
key_discriminators: Fill pattern: solid oval (person) vs torus/ring with dark centre (life ring)
Bilateral symmetry: present along body axis (person) vs absent or circular (life ring)
Spatial context: loose vessel cluster in SAR scenario strongly favours PIW
Long-axis proportion: slightly elongated (person head+shoulders) vs near-circular (life ring)
confusable_pair_notes.resolution_notes: At scout tier (1-2 cm/pixel, no stabilisation, wave blur), the dark central void of a life ring may not be resolved. Bilateral-symmetry precondition removed from scout rule per grounding check. Commander tier (0.5-1 cm/pixel, stabilised gimbal) can reliably resolve both the central void and bilateral symmetry.
